body_paragraphs: The oil story hasn't cooled off since this morning - it's gotten louder. The U.S. carried out its eleventh consecutive night of strikes on Iran, and Brent crude pushed past $92, up from $91.10 at Tuesday's close. Markets shrugged for about half a session: the Dow and S&P are up modestly today while the Nasdaq is essentially flat, with Alphabet and Tesla earnings after the close doing more to hold everyone's attention than the war is. Fed hike odds haven't moved from Tuesday's 16.6%, which means the market is still treating oil as a slow-burn risk rather than an emergency - a read that gets tested every night the strikes continue.

The Marlins' skid is confirmed at eight straight, and the final score deserves a correction from what circulated this morning: Houston won 5-3, not 5-8, with Jeremy Pena's leadoff homer and a three-run shot from Isaac Paredes doing the damage before Joe Mack's seventh-inning homer made it respectable. Sandy Alcantara takes the mound tonight in Houston looking to snap it. Meanwhile the Dolphins' rebuild keeps its slow, deliberate shape - rookies are in, veterans arrive next Tuesday, and the first joint practice (Aug. 12, at Washington) is now on the calendar.

Otherwise, today asks less of you than most. The rest-ethic piece you starred this morning is worth sitting with again tonight, and the CBRE capital markets report you saved is queued for the weekend, right where it belongs.

what_changed.title: Strikes reach an eleventh straight night as oil tops $92
what_changed.previous_state: As of Tuesday, the U.S. had carried out roughly nine nights of strikes, with Brent at $91.10.
what_changed.current_state: The U.S. has now struck Iran for eleven consecutive nights; Brent crude is above $92 and reviving inflation concern.
what_changed.why_it_matters: Each additional night narrows the odds that the 10-day ceasefire proposal lands before it's overtaken by events.

what_changed.title: Eighth straight loss confirmed, final score corrected
what_changed.previous_state: This morning's edition listed Tuesday's final as 5-8.
what_changed.current_state: The confirmed final was Houston 5, Miami 3 - Pena and Paredes homered, Imai worked six strong innings, and Joe Mack's two-run homer in the seventh made it look closer than it was. The eight-game skid itself is unchanged and confirmed by multiple box scores.
what_changed.why_it_matters: Same losing streak, more accurate picture of how these games are actually being lost - starting pitching is mostly holding; the deficit comes from a handful of mistake pitches.

themes.title: Eleven nights in, the ceasefire math gets harder
themes.thesis: Every additional night of strikes is a night the 10-day ceasefire proposal isn't being tested.
themes.synthesis: The U.S. carried out its eleventh consecutive night of strikes on Iran as of Wednesday, and Brent crude pushed past $92 - up from $91.10 at Tuesday's close - reviving inflation concerns that had briefly taken a back seat to chip-earnings optimism. The Qatar-Egypt-Pakistan ceasefire proposal from earlier this week hasn't visibly slowed anything on the ground. Hegseth's $37.5B war-cost estimate from Tuesday still stands as the most recent official figure.
themes.new_today: The strike count moved from nine nights (Tuesday) to eleven (Wednesday), and Brent moved from $91.10 to above $92.
themes.why_it_matters: Every day this runs past the FOMC meeting is another day oil is doing the Fed's job for it - just not in a way anyone would choose - and the gap between the diplomatic track and the strikes is now wide enough to notice.

sources.headline: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq waver with Alphabet, Tesla earnings on deck
U.S. strikes Iran and Houthis threaten Saudi Arabia shipping as mediators push 10-day ceasefire

themes.title: A quieter session, the same unresolved question
themes.thesis: Tuesday's rally didn't carry through - which is itself informative.
themes.synthesis: After Tuesday's chip-led rally (S&P +0.89%, Nasdaq +1.29%), Wednesday's session has been more subdued: the Dow up around 0.3%, the S&P roughly flat to slightly positive, and the Nasdaq giving back a touch as chip momentum fades and oil climbs. Alphabet and Tesla report after today's close, which is likely doing more to hold the market in place than the Iran story is. FOMC hike odds are unchanged at 16.6% (83.4% hold) heading into next week's meeting.
themes.new_today: The rally didn't extend into Wednesday; earnings, not the war, are now the session's proximate driver.
themes.why_it_matters: For CRE debt costs and cap rates, a market that's stopped reacting to daily oil headlines is either calmer or complacent - worth watching which one it turns out to be after Thursday's Big Tech prints.

sources.headline: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq waver with Alphabet, Tesla earnings on deck

themes.title: Eight straight - and the box score says it's still close games, not blowouts
themes.thesis: The streak is real, but these are one-swing losses, not collapses.
themes.synthesis: Houston beat Miami 5-3 Tuesday - the Marlins' eighth straight loss and the franchise's longest skid since April 2024. Jeremy Pena's leadoff homer and Isaac Paredes' three-run shot did the damage; Tatsuya Imai worked six strong innings for Houston, but Joe Mack's two-run homer in the seventh kept it from getting away entirely. Max Meyer remains on the 15-day IL with a neck strain, his fifth straight season with an IL trip. Sandy Alcantara starts tonight's series finale looking to end the streak.
themes.new_today: Final score confirmed as 5-3, not 5-8; box score detail (Pena, Paredes, Mack home runs) now available.
themes.why_it_matters: Eight losses by a run or two apiece is a fixable problem in a way that eight blowouts wouldn't be - tonight's game is a real test of whether this roster still believes that.

sources.headline: Phillips settles in, but early trouble pushes Marlins' skid to 8 straight
MLB Gameday: Marlins 3, Astros 5 Final Score (07/21/2026)

themes.title: The rebuild just got its third anchor
themes.thesis: A patience-year roster doesn't lock up a linebacker through 2029 unless it means it.
themes.synthesis: Miami signed Jordyn Brooks to a three-year, $51.3 million extension ($35M guaranteed) Tuesday, keeping him through 2029 - the rebuild's third pillar signing after Achane and Brewer. Rookies reported this week; veterans arrive July 28. The first joint practice is now set for Aug. 12 at Washington, with the Giants visiting Miami Gardens Aug. 20.
themes.new_today: Joint-practice schedule (Aug. 12 at Washington, Aug. 20 hosting the Giants) is now confirmed.
themes.why_it_matters: This is the tell for how serious the Hafley regime is about its timeline - you don't guarantee $35M to a linebacker in a true tank year.

sources.headline: Report: Dolphins LB Jordyn Brooks agrees to three-year, $51.3M extension
Key Miami Dolphins NFL training camp, joint practice dates released

themes.title: An eight-story bet on a $1 billion neighborhood
themes.thesis: The stadium got the headlines; the civic center next door is the more interesting government story.
themes.synthesis: Miami topped off an eight-story, 257,000-square-foot Civic Center at Miami Freedom Park on July 10, days after securing a $193 million construction permit. The building - developed by Adler Development, built by Moss Construction - is part of the 130-acre, $1 billion Freedom Park project anchored by Inter Miami's stadium, which also includes a $20 million pedestrian bridge from the Metrorail station. Completion is targeted for spring 2028.
themes.new_today: No new developments today; carried forward as still relevant and unresolved.
themes.why_it_matters: A real, financed, under-construction civic asset next to a private stadium deal - worth tracking as a model for how the city pairs public buildings with private development.

sources.headline: Miami secures $193M permit to build civic center at Miami Freedom Park

themes.title: The summer list is almost fully checked off
themes.thesis: The anticipated list from three weeks ago is nearly exhausted - which means it's time to look at fall's.
themes.synthesis: Manoli opened in Coconut Grove this week, alongside Crisppi's, Redhead Burger (Doral), Star Burger, and Flight West in South Miami. That leaves La Sponda and PopUp Bagels as the only holdovers from the original summer list still pending. Fall's list is already forming: Genghis Cohen, Royale Pizza Napoletana, Miami Slice, and Levain Bakery's first Miami location, opening in Coconut Grove.
themes.new_today: No new developments today; carried forward.
themes.why_it_matters: Worth booking the summer stragglers before attention - and reservations - shift to the fall names.

sources.headline: New Miami restaurant openings and closings this week

themes.title: Rest is not the wellness problem it's marketed as
themes.thesis: The Stoics didn't treat rest as indulgence - they treated it as maintenance.
themes.synthesis: A cluster of recent Stoic writing converges on the same corrective: burnout isn't fixed by meditation apps or boundary-setting scripts, it's a signal that effort is going somewhere that doesn't compound. The Stoic frame treats deliberate rest, reflection, and reading as part of the discipline itself, not a break from it - what one recent piece called a resting ethic rather than a reward earned by output.
themes.new_today: Nothing new to report; you starred this one this morning, so it's carried forward and weighted up rather than replaced.
themes.why_it_matters: You flagged operating at redline this week - this is the piece worth actually re-reading tonight, not just starring.

sources.headline: Finding Your Pace: Stoic Advice for Dealing with Burnout
How to Prevent & Overcome Burnout - by Stoic Philosophy

themes.title: Plastic that eats itself in six days
themes.thesis: The most useful materials science this month solves a problem by building in its own ending.
themes.synthesis: Researchers at the Chinese University of Hong Kong embedded dormant Bacillus subtilis spores into a common 3D-printing polymer. Left alone, the plastic behaves normally; heated, the spores wake up and secrete two cooperating enzymes - one that chops the polymer into pieces, one that chews those pieces into base molecules - breaking the material down completely in six days with no microplastic residue.
themes.new_today: No new developments today; carried forward.
themes.why_it_matters: A small, concrete example of solving a waste problem by design rather than by better recycling - worth noticing precisely because it isn't trying to be a headline.

sources.headline: New living plastic self-destructs in just 6 days without leaving microplastics
Bacteria-boosted living plastic breaks down on command

beautiful_thing.title: Quipu Gut
beautiful_thing.creator: Cecilia Vicuna
beautiful_thing.body: A 30-foot column of dyed red, unspun wool - fifty strands, knotted rhythmically - hanging from the double-height ceiling of PAMM's main gallery. Vicuna, a Chilean poet and artist born in Santiago in 1948, built the piece around the quipu, the knotted-cord system Andean societies used to record information for centuries before European contact. It was first shown at documenta 14 in Kassel, Germany, alongside a video of Vicuna and her crew performing the ritual steps of the installation, chanting as they worked. It closes at PAMM on August 9.

noticing_prompt.text: Notice one thing today you kept carrying only because putting it down felt like it wasn't allowed - not because it still needed carrying.

evening_quote.text: People look for retreats for themselves, in the country, by the coast, or in the hills... There is nowhere that a person can find a more peaceful and trouble-free retreat than in his own mind.

threads.title: US-Iran conflict and the Strait of Hormuz
threads.current_state: The U.S. has now struck Iran for eleven consecutive nights. Brent crude is above $92, up from $91.10 Tuesday. The Qatar/Egypt/Pakistan 10-day ceasefire proposal has not visibly slowed the strikes. Hegseth's $37.5B war-cost estimate from Tuesday still stands as the latest figure.

timeline.summary: First tracked: nine nights of US strikes, oil up about 20% this month, 10-day ceasefire being mediated.
10-day ceasefire formally proposed by Qatar/Egypt/Pakistan; US strikes continue, Iran calls it full-scale war, Brent tops $91, war cost estimated at $37.5B.
By Wednesday, strikes reach an eleventh consecutive night; Brent pushes past $92, reviving inflation concern.

threads.title: Federal Reserve rate path
threads.current_state: FOMC meets July 28-29. Hike odds unchanged at 16.6% (83.4% hold) as of Wednesday. 10Y at 4.60%. Tuesday's chip-led rally (S&P +0.89%, Nasdaq +1.29%) did not carry into Wednesday, which is running more subdued as oil climbs and traders await Alphabet and Tesla earnings after today's close.

timeline.summary: Hike odds rising toward 46%; BofA sees three hikes by December.
Fed week arrives; positioning visible in Friday's chip-led selloff.
Hike odds collapse to 13.3% (86.7% hold) amid Iran-driven oil shock; 10Y ticks up to 4.59%.
Hike odds tick back up to 16.6% as oil keeps climbing; stocks rally anyway on chip earnings; June CPI confirmed at 3.5%.
Wednesday's session runs subdued (Dow +0.3%, S&P roughly flat, Nasdaq slightly negative) as oil-driven inflation concern resurfaces ahead of Big Tech earnings; hike odds unchanged at 16.6%.
